skill.name: video-edit
skill.description: Edit existing video on RunComfy — this skill is a smart router that matches the user's intent to the right edit model in the RunComfy catalog. Picks Wan 2.7 Edit-Video (general restyle / background swap / packaging swap, identity + motion preservation), Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control (transfer precise motion from a reference video to a target character), or Lucy Edit Restyle (lightweight identity-stable restyle / outfit swap). Bundles each model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper edits without burning iterations on the wrong model. Calls `runcomfy run <vendor>/<model>/<endpoint>` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "video edit", "edit video", "restyle video", "swap video background", "motion control", "outfit swap video", or any explicit ask to transform a video.
skill.summary: Intent-routed video editing skill that routes to the right AI model for restyle, motion transfer, or background swaps.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 101 static findings are false positives. The skill is markdown documentation for a video editing CLI skill. External command flags (66) misidentified bash code fences as shell execution. Network flags (14) are legitimate service URLs. Filesystem flags (9) reference documentation placeholders and token storage paths. No actual security risks exist in this content.

content.user_title: Edit Videos with AI-Powered Model Routing
content.value_statement: Video editing requires choosing the right AI model for each task. This skill routes requests to Wan 2.7 for restyle, Kling 2.6 for motion transfer, or Lucy for lightweight edits—eliminating trial-and-error model selection.
actual_capabilities: Route video edit requests to Wan 2.7 Edit-Video for identity-preserving restyle and background swaps
Transfer precise motion from a reference video to a target character using Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control
Apply lightweight identity-stable edits like outfit swaps and atmospheric restyling via Lucy Edit Restyle
Build properly formatted JSON prompts for each model including preservation goals and edit directions
Handle output downloads and manage the RunComfy CLI workflow end-to-end
Preserve source audio when specified using the audio_setting parameter
limitations: Each edit must use one model per call—no multi-model blending
Output duration and resolution limits vary by model: Wan 2.7 supports up to 1080p and 10s, Lucy caps at 720p
No aspect ratio control on Lucy route—output matches input automatically
Compound edits across multiple directions tend to drift on motion

use_cases.title: Product Video Background Replacement
use_cases.description: Swap the background of a product demonstration video while preserving camera motion and hand movements during the demo.

use_cases.title: Lip Sync Animation from Reference
use_cases.description: Transfer lip movement from a source video to an animated character image, creating natural lip-sync without manual keyframing.

use_cases.title: Actor Outfit Change in Scene
use_cases.description: Change an actor's costume in an existing scene while keeping their face identity and natural motion intact.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Background Swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the background of my video to a modern office. Keep the speaker's face, pose, and lip movement exactly the same.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to change where someone appears without changing them

prompt_templates.title: Motion Transfer to Character
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the dancing motion from [reference video] to the character in [character image]. Keep original sound.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to animate a still image with motion from a video

prompt_templates.title: Packaging Design Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the packaging design shown in the video using [reference image]. Keep original framing and hand movements.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a video showing a product and wants updated packaging

prompt_templates.title: Atmospheric Restyle
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the lighting to warm golden hour. Preserve face, pose, and natural motion throughout.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to change the mood or lighting of a video

output_examples.input: Swap the background to a beach sunset while keeping the person's movement
output_examples.output: Background changes from original setting to beach sunset with preserved speaker identity and motion. Audio remains from original.

output_examples.input: Make the character dance with the same moves as the reference video
output_examples.output: Static character image animates with dancing motion from reference video, maintaining character appearance and outputting with original sound.

best_practices: Lead with preservation goals in prompts: "Preserve [face/pose/lip movement]; then [state the change]"
Use one edit direction per call to avoid motion drift and maintain quality
Apply reference_image only for direct design or packaging transfers, not general restyling
anti_patterns: Avoid compound edits in a single prompt—split into multiple sequential calls instead
Do not use Lucy for full character replacement—it is built for localized style modifications only
Skip over-specifying preservation; simple "preserve face and motion" often works better than detailed lists

faq.question: Which model should I use for a talking-head video restyle?
faq.answer: Use Wan 2.7 Edit-Video. It provides strong identity and motion preservation for talking-head content up to 1080p resolution.

faq.question: How do I transfer motion from one video to a character image?
faq.answer: Use Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control with character_orientation set to "image". The reference video provides motion, the image provides the character. Output caps at 10 seconds.

faq.question: Can I edit video background and change clothing in one call?
faq.answer: No. Each call routes to one model with one edit direction. Compound edits drift on motion. Make separate sequential calls for each change.

faq.question: What video formats and sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Wan 2.7 accepts MP4/MOV at 2–10 seconds and up to 100MB. Kling accepts 10–30 second videos depending on orientation. Lucy supports MP4/MOV/WEBM/GIF at 720p max.

faq.question: How do I preserve the original audio?
faq.answer: Set audio_setting to "origin" for Wan 2.7, or keep_original_sound to true for Kling. Lucy does not have audio preservation options.

faq.question: What resolution does each model support?
faq.answer: Wan 2.7 supports 720p or 1080p. Kling 2.6 inherits input resolution. Lucy Edit Restyle is capped at 720p with no aspect ratio control.
